Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Radiology / Imaging Report for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Tamil Nadu, district=Vellore.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Tamil for Tamil.

PLACEMENT: Include [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] as the reporting radiologist (e.g. Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]]) — do not leave the doctor name as untagged prose.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 48
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Picker (Tobacco)
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Vellore
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: Senniyapp Kumar, a 48-year-old male, is a tobacco picker in rural Tamil Nadu, currently married, with an illiterate education level. He resides in Vellore district and speaks Tamil as his first language. His lifestyle includes manual labor, community involvement, and a persistent anxiety about financial stability.

Document format guide for Radiology / Imaging Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Findings: <radiology prose, untagged>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

கதிரியக்க அறிக்கை — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College Hospital, Vellore]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Senniyapp Kumar]] [[AGE|48]] [[GENDER|Male]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0815-001]] | டாக்டர் [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Ramesh Kumar]] மூலம் தெரிவிக்கப்பட்டது
கண்டறிதல்கள்: PA மார்பு கதிரியக்கப்படத்தில் மேல் மடல் ஆதிக்கம் செலுத்தும் வகையில் பல இருபக்க நுரையீரல் ஊடுருவல்கள் காணப்படுகின்றன. வலது மேல் பகுதியில் சுமார் 2.5 செ.மீ. விட்டம் கொண்ட குழி போன்ற புண்கள் கவனிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளன. வலது பக்கத்தில் தொடர்புடைய நுரையீரல் உறை தடிப்பு உள்ளது. ஹிலார் நிணநீர் முடிச்சு வீக்கம் தெளிவாகத் தெரிகிறது; ஹிலார் நிழல்கள் இருபக்கமும் பெரிதாகி காணப்படுகின்றன. இதயத்தின் நிழல் வடிவம் அளவு சீராக உள்ளது; இதய வீக்கம் (கார்டியோமெகலி) இருப்பதற்கான சான்றுகள் இல்லை. எலும்பு அமைப்புகள் முழுமையாக உள்ளன; எலும்பு அரிப்பு புண்கள் (லைடிக் லீஷன்ஸ்) ஏதுமில்லை. நுரையீரல் உறை நீர் கோர்த்தல் (ப்ளூரல் எஃப்யூஷன்) ஏதுமில்லை.

முடிவு: கண்டறிதல்கள் குழி நோய் (கேவிடரி டிசீஸ்) உடன் கூடிய செயலில் உள்ள நுரையீரல் காசநோயுடன் ஒத்துப்போகின்றன. மேல் மடல் ஆதிக்கம் மற்றும் குழி புண்கள் மீண்டும் செயல்படும் காசநோயை (ரியாக்டிவேஷன் டிபி) உணர்த்துகின்றன. இதய வீக்கம் அல்லது தீவிர நுரையீரல் நீர் கோர்த்தல் (அக்யூட் பல்மோனரி எடிமா) இருப்பதற்கான உடனடி சான்றுகள் இல்லை. தேசிய வழிகாட்டுதல்களின்படி, AFB ஸ்மியர் மற்றும் கல்ச்சருக்காக சளி பரிசோதனை மற்றும் காசநோய் எதிர்ப்பு சிகிச்சையைத் தொடங்குமாறு பரிந்துரைக்கப்படுகிறது. சிகிச்சைக்கான எதிர்வினையை மதிப்பிட 2 மாதங்களில் தொடர் மார்பு கதிரியக்கப்படம் எடுக்கப்பட வேண்டும்.

[[DISTRICT|Vellore]] [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] [[HOSPITAL_ID|GMCV-001]] [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
payload.generated_text_en:
Radiology Report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College Hospital, Vellore]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Senniyapp Kumar]] [[AGE|48]] [[GENDER|Male]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0815-001]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Ramesh Kumar]]
Findings: PA chest radiograph reveals multiple bilateral pulmonary infiltrates with upper lobe predominance. There are cavitary lesions noted in the right upper zone measuring approximately 2.5 cm in diameter. Associated pleural thickening is present on the right side. Hilar lymphadenopathy is evident with bilateral enlargement of the hilar shadows. The cardiac silhouette appears normal in size with no evidence of cardiomegaly. The bony structures are intact without any lytic lesions. No pleural effusion is identified.

Impression: Findings are consistent with active pulmonary tuberculosis with cavitary disease. Upper lobe predominance and cavitary lesions suggest reactivation TB. No immediate evidence of cardiac enlargement or acute pulmonary edema. Recommend sputum examination for AFB smear and culture, and initiation of anti-tubercular therapy as per national guidelines. Follow-up chest radiograph in 2 months to assess treatment response.

[[DISTRICT|Vellore]] [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] [[HOSPITAL_ID|GMCV-001]] [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Radiology / Imaging Report for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Gujarati (code=gu, script=Gujarati). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Gujarat, district=Bharuch.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Gujarati for Gujarati.

PLACEMENT: Include [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] as the reporting radiologist (e.g. Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]]) — do not leave the doctor name as untagged prose.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 19
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Building Construction Labourers, Other
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Urban
- state: Gujarat
- district: Bharuch
- first_language: Gujarati
- clinical_persona_summary: A 19-year-old male construction labourer from Bharuch, Gujarat, with middle education, who is never married and lives in an urban setting. His first language is Gujarati, and he works in construction, showing a methodical approach and competitive drive. He reports occasional back aches and frugal habits, with a diet rich in local street foods like dhokla and fafda.

Document format guide for Radiology / Imaging Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Findings: <radiology prose, untagged>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

દર્દી છેલ્લા ત્રણ મહિનાથી પેટના નીચેના જમણા ભાગમાં તૂટક તૂટક, મંદ દુખાવો, અને છેલ્લા ત્રણ મહિનામાં અંદાજે પાંચ કિલો વજન ઘટવાની ફરિયાદ સાથે રજૂ થયા છે. ક્યારેક હળવો તાવ પણ આવે છે. આહારમાં કોઈ ફેરફાર કે તાજેતરમાં કોઈ ચેપ લાગ્યો નથી.

તારણો:
પેટ અને પેલ્વિસનું MRI કરવામાં આવ્યું હતું. તારણો: જમણા ઇલિયાક ફોસામાં 4.5 cm નું વિષમ દળ (mass) જોવા મળ્યું છે, જે ટર્મિનલ ઇલિયમમાંથી ઉદ્ભવે છે. આ દળ T1-વેઇટેડ ઇમેજ પર મધ્યવર્તી સંકેત અને T2-વેઇટેડ ઇમેજ પર ઉચ્ચ સંકેત દર્શાવે છે, જેમાં નેક્રોસિસના વિસ્તારો છે. તેની સાથે પ્રાદેશિક લિમ્ફેડેનોપેથી પણ જોવા મળી છે. ગેડોલિનિયમ આપ્યા પછી આ દળમાં વધારો (enhancement) જોવા મળે છે. દૂરના મેટાસ્ટેસિસ અથવા એસાઇટ્સના કોઈ પુરાવા નથી. પેટના અને પેલ્વિસના અન્ય અંગો સામાન્ય છે.

છાપ:
1. જમણા ઇલિયાક ફોસામાં મોટું વિષમ દળ, સંભવતઃ નાના આંતરડામાંથી ઉદ્ભવેલું (દા.ત., લિમ્ફોમા અથવા ગેસ્ટ્રોઇન્ટેસ્ટાઇનલ સ્ટ્રોમલ ટ્યુમર - GIST).
2. સંબંધિત પ્રાદેશિક લિમ્ફેડેનોપેથી.
3. તારણો પ્રાથમિક પેટના કેન્સર માટે ચિંતાજનક છે.

યોજના:
તારણો કેન્સર માટે ખૂબ જ શંકાસ્પદ છે. ટ્યુમર માર્કર્સ (દા.ત., CEA, CA 19-9, AFP, beta-hCG) સાથે સહસંબંધની ભલામણ કરવામાં આવે છે. હિસ્ટોપેથોલોજિકલ નિદાન માટે દળનું CT-ગાઇડેડ કોર નીડલ બાયોપ્સીની ભલામણ કરવામાં આવે છે. વધુ વ્યવસ્થાપન માટે, જેમાં સ્ટેજિંગ વર્કઅપ (દા.ત., PET-CT સ્કેન) અને સારવારના વિકલ્પો (કેમોથેરાપી, સર્જરી, વગેરે) ની ચર્ચાનો સમાવેશ થાય છે, ઓન્કોલોજી વિભાગમાં રેફરલની ભલામણ કરવામાં આવે છે. દર્દીને તારણો અને વધુ તપાસની જરૂરિયાત વિશે સલાહ આપવામાં આવી છે.

[[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
સંપર્ક નંબર: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]].

MRN [[MRN|MRN-GJ-2024-00123]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-GJ-2024-05-001]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Shah]]

Patient presented with a history of chronic low back pain for the past six months, exacerbated by heavy physical labor. The pain is dull and aching, radiating to the buttocks, with occasional numbness in the left leg.

Findings:
MRI of the lumbar spine reveals multilevel degenerative disc disease. There is a posterolateral disc protrusion at the L4-L5 level, causing mild compression of the left traversing L5 nerve root. The L5-S1 disc shows desiccation with a small Schmorl's node. There is mild spondylolisthesis of L4 over L5, grade I. The spinal canal is adequate, and there is no significant neural element compression at the central canal. The facet joints show mild arthropathy. No acute fracture or abnormal marrow signal is identified. The vertebral bodies and posterior elements are otherwise unremarkable.

Impression:
1.  L4-L5 posterolateral disc protrusion with left L5 nerve root irritation.
2.  Multilevel lumbar degenerative disc disease.
3.  Grade I L4-L5 spondylolisthesis.

Plan:
The findings are consistent with chronic mechanical low back pain secondary to occupational stress and early degenerative changes. Conservative management is recommended, including a course of physiotherapy focusing on core strengthening and posture correction. A trial of oral analgesics and anti-inflammatory medication is advised. The patient is advised to avoid heavy lifting and strenuous activity for the next four weeks. A follow-up clinical review in four weeks is scheduled to assess symptom response. An epidural steroid injection may be considered if conservative measures fail.

Contact number: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]].

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

LAB REPORT
[[HOSPITAL_NAME|Kohima District Hospital]]
[[DISTRICT|Kohima]]

Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
Ordered by [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Kevinuo K. Jamir]]

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Patient presents with a two-month history of persistent worry, low mood, and decreased energy.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Mary Lotha]] is a 48-year-old female, a bill and accounts collector, who reports increasing difficulty managing stress at work. She describes constant feelings of apprehension, restlessness, and an inability to relax. Sleep is disturbed with early morning awakening and difficulty falling back asleep. Appetite is slightly reduced. She reports a lack of interest in her usual solitary morning hikes. No reported suicidal or homicidal ideation.

PAST PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY:
No prior psychiatric diagnosis or treatment.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
Hypertension, diagnosed one year ago, managed with medication.

MEDICATIONS:
Amlodipine 5mg once daily.

FAMILY HISTORY:
Non-contributory for psychiatric illness.

SOCIAL HISTORY:
Patient is currently married and lives with her spouse in Kohima. She works as a bill and accounts collector. She is fluent in Nepali and conversational in Hindi. She enjoys well-planned solo trips.

MENTAL STATUS EXAMINATION:
Appearance: Well-groomed, appropriate for age and gender.
Behavior: Cooperative and alert.
Speech: Normal rate and rhythm.
Mood: Anxious.
Affect: Congruent but constricted.
Thought Process: Logical and goal-directed.
Thought Content: Denies delusions, hallucinations, or paranoia.
Insight: Fair.
Judgment: Fair.

ASSESSMENT:
1. Generalized Anxiety Disorder, Moderate.
2. Major Depressive Disorder, Single Episode, Mild.
3. Hypertension.

PLAN:
1. Continue Amlodipine 5mg daily.
2. Start Sertraline 50mg once daily.
3. Counsel patient on stress management techniques and importance of sleep hygiene.
4. Refer to the Behavioral Health Clinic for Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT).
5. Follow up in 4 weeks to assess response to treatment.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
Date of Collection: 15 August 2024

Vitamin D, 25-OH | 18 ng/mL | 30 - 100 ng/mL
HbA1c | 5.4% | <5.7%
Liver Function Tests (LFT) | Within Normal Limits | -
Kidney Function Tests (KFT) | Within Normal Limits | -

payload.travel_persona: Senniyapp Kumar prefers short trips by bus to nearby towns like Chennai and Thiruvannamalai for temple pilgrimages, planning each journey with simple budgets and staying in modest guesthouses, while they dream of a future road trip to the hills of Ooty.
payload.culinary_persona: Senniyapp Kumar prefers simple home‑cooked meals like sambar with rice, rasam, idli, and traditional pongal, enjoys the aroma of fresh curry leaves and mustard seeds, and they occasionally prepare milagu podi for a spicy kick.
payload.persona: Senniyapp Kumar is a diligent tobacco picker with a competitive streak, strong community ties, lingering anxiety, and a dream of owning land while they juggle family responsibilities.
payload.cultural_background: Senniyapp Kumar comes from a Tamil-speaking agricultural community on the outskirts of Vellore in rural Tamil Nadu. His childhood was rooted in traditional Tamil customs such as Pongal celebrations, village temple rituals, and the joint-family system. The local economy revolves around farming and tobacco cultivation, which has given him a practical, hands-on approach to life. Social gatherings, community festivals and village committees provide a structured yet flexible social framework that matches his preference for predictability while allowing him to enjoy group activities.
payload.linguistic_background: Senniyapp's mother tongue is Tamil, which he speaks fluently and uses for everyday communication at home and in the fields. He relies on oral instructions, practical demonstration, and memorised procedures to acquire new skills.

He possesses strong manual dexterity, physical stamina to endure long hours in the fields, and an intuitive sense for assessing leaf quality. He can estimate yields, keep simple tallies, and coordinate with other pickers to ensure efficient harvesting. His familiarity with market demands also allows him to negotiate basic terms with local buyers.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Tobacco leaf picking", "Leaf grading", "Manual dexterity", "Physical stamina", "Team coordination", "Basic market negotiation"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: In his free time, Senniyapp enjoys playing cricket with village friends, listening to Tamil folk songs and devotional music, and attending local temple festivals. He likes watching Tamil movies on the community television and participating in traditional dance gatherings during Pongal. He also enjoys simple cooking of regional dishes and occasional storytelling sessions (kathakalakshepa) with elders.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Playing cricket", "Listening to Tamil folk and devotional music", "Watching Tamil movies", "Participating in temple festivals", "Cooking regional dishes", "Storytelling sessions"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Senniyapp aspires to secure a stable and higher-earning position, ideally becoming a supervisor or foreman in the tobacco-picking crews. He hopes to save enough to purchase a modest piece of land for his own small-scale farming, which would give him more control over his work schedule and income. Improving his own basic literacy and managing his anxiety are personal goals he wishes to achieve to increase his self-reliance.

His primary language is Tamil, and he works in the agricultural sector, coordinating harvest teams and aspiring to become a foreman. He experiences anxiety and dreams of owning land while managing family responsibilities.

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete ER Triage & Nursing Notes for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Tamil Nadu, district=Vellore.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Tamil for Tamil.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once in the arrival/RTA/ambulance line. Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 48
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Picker (Tobacco)
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Vellore
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: Senniyapp Kumar is a 48-year-old male tobacco picker from rural Vellore, Tamil Nadu, who is currently married and has an illiterate education level. His primary language is Tamil, and he works in the agricultural sector, coordinating harvest teams and aspiring to become a foreman. He experiences anxiety and dreams of owning land while managing family responsibilities.

Document format guide for ER Triage & Nursing Notes (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|...]]
Arrived by ambulance / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|...]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Vitals / acuity: <short notes>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.

- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).

payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Dogri (code=doi, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Jammu & Kashmir, district=Reasi.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Dogri.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 26
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Rural
- state: Jammu & Kashmir
- district: Reasi
- first_language: Dogri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 26-year-old male currently married, retired homemaker from Reasi, Jammu & Kashmir, with a middle education level, speaks Dogri as his first language and Hindi functionally. He resides in a rural zone and manages household finances while engaging in digital learning and gardening. His lifestyle includes morning walks, occasional festival sweets, and a diet rich in Dogri staples like Kaladi cheese and Aloo Madra.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

payload.travel_persona: Chanda Pathak prefers modest road trips to nearby destinations like Shillong, Kaziranga National Park, and Gangtok, often traveling by shared jeeps, while dreaming of a future visit to Kerala’s backwaters and the tea gardens of Darjeeling, and they eagerly plan each journey with a balance of curiosity and budgeting.
payload.culinary_persona: Chanda Pathak skillfully prepares traditional dishes like onla (bamboo shoot stew) and lahara (fermented rice), loves the tangy flavor of mustard greens, often adds fresh ginger and mustard oil, and enjoys occasional meals of Assamese masor tenga with lemon and tomato, and they cherish sharing these meals with family during communal gatherings.
payload.persona: Chanda Pathak balances relentless budgeting and worry with a love for weaving colorful handloom fabrics, community dances, and early‑morning river walks, thriving on routine yet yearning for broader horizons.
payload.cultural_background: Chanda hails from the Bodo community in rural Kokrajhar, Assam. Her upbringing blends traditional Bodo customs—such as the Bwisagu festival, folk dances, and handloom weaving—with the broader Assamese rural culture and Hindu rites. Growing up in an agrarian village with close‑knit joint families, she internalises values of community harmony, respect for nature, and practical self‑reliance.
payload.linguistic_background: Her mother tongue is Bodo, which she speaks fluently and can read and write using the Devanagari script adopted for Bodo. She has functional literacy in Assamese, the regional lingua franca, and can understand basic Hindi and simple English taught at middle school. She uses Hindi for religious and occasional administrative purposes, though not with full fluency.
payload.skills_and_expertise: She excels at household management, including budgeting, meal planning, and maintaining a hygienic environment. She is proficient in preparing traditional Bodo dishes such as onla and lahara, and in weaving Bodo textiles using a handloom. She also possesses basic first‑aid knowledge, literacy in Bodo and Assamese, and community volunteer experience organising village festivals and cultural events.

payload.hobbies_and_interests: She enjoys listening to Bodo folk songs, practising the traditional Bodo dance (Wapung), and weaving colourful handloom fabrics. In her spare time, she likes gardening, reading simple Assamese stories, and participating in village fairs. She also finds solace in nature walks along the Brahmaputra’s tributaries.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Bodo folk music", "Practicing Wapung dance", "Handloom weaving", "Gardening", "Reading simple Assamese stories", "Attending village fairs", "Nature walks along riverbanks"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Chanda aspires to enhance her family's well‑being by acquiring formal training in early childhood education or community health. She aims to start a small home‑based venture making Bodo handloom products to generate supplemental income. payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 20-year-old female from rural Kokrajhar, Assam, currently married, with a middle education level, who is a homemaker. Her first language is Bodo, and she has functional literacy in Assamese. Clinically, she exhibits a routine-oriented lifestyle with a history of meticulous budgeting and community-oriented activities like handloom weaving.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:

நோயாளி: பொதுவாக இது மந்தமான வலியாகவே இருக்கிறது, ஆனால் நான் இருமும்போதோ அல்லது சிரமப்படும்போதோ அது கூர்மையான வலியாக மாறுகிறது. சில நேரங்களில் அந்த வீக்கத்தை நான் மீண்டும் உள்ளே தள்ள முடியும், ஆனால் அது மீண்டும் வெளியே வருகிறது.
மருத்துவர் [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: விளக்கியதற்கு நன்றி. நீங்கள் விவரிப்பது இடுப்புப் பகுதி குடலிறக்கத்தைப் போலவே இருக்கிறது. உங்கள் வயிற்றுத் தசைகளில் உள்ள ஒரு பலவீனமான இடத்தில் உங்கள் குடலின் ஒரு பகுதி தள்ளப்படும்போது இது நிகழ்கிறது.
நோயாளி: குடலிறக்கமா? மருத்துவரே, இது மிகவும் தீவிரமானதா? எனக்கு அறுவை சிகிச்சை தேவையா?
மருத்துவர் [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: ஆம், அதைச் சரிசெய்ய உங்களுக்கு ஒரு சிறிய அறுவை சிகிச்சை தேவைப்படலாம். முக்கிய ஆபத்து என்னவென்றால், அந்த வீக்கம் சிக்கிக்கொண்டு இரத்த ஓட்டம் துண்டிக்கப்படுவதுதான், இது ஒரு மருத்துவ அவசரநிலை. அது நடப்பதைத் தடுக்க வேண்டும்.
நோயாளி: செலவைப் பற்றியும், எனது வேலையிலிருந்து விடுப்பு எடுப்பது பற்றியும் நான் கவலைப்படுகிறேன். நான் தான் குடும்பத்தின் முதன்மை வருமானம் ஈட்டுபவர்.
மருத்துவர் [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: உங்கள் கவலைகளை நான் புரிந்துகொள்கிறேன். மாவட்ட மருத்துவமனை வேலூரில் ஒரு பொது அறுவை சிகிச்சைத் துறை உள்ளது. அவர்கள் இத்தகைய பல அறுவை சிகிச்சைகளைச் செய்கிறார்கள், உங்களைப் போன்ற நோயாளிகளுக்குச் செலவு மிகக் குறைவாக இருக்கும். மருத்துவமனையின் சமூகப் பணியாளரிடம் நீங்கள் பேசலாம், அவர்கள் உங்கள் சேர்க்கைக்கும் உங்களுக்குத் தேவைப்படும் நிதி உதவிக்கும் உதவுவார்கள். இப்போதைக்கு, நீங்கள் கனமான எடைகளைத் தூக்குவதை நிறுத்த வேண்டும் மற்றும் கடினமான செயல்பாடுகளைத் தவிர்க்க வேண்டும்.
நோயாளி: சரி மருத்துவரே. நான் நாளை மருத்துவமனைக்குச் செல்வேன். அதற்கும் உள்ளே நான் என்ன செய்ய வேண்டும்?
மருத்துவர் [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: முடிந்தவரை ஓய்வெடுக்க முயலுங்கள். உங்களுக்குக் கடுமையான வலி, காய்ச்சல் ஏற்பட்டால், அல்லது வீக்கம் கடினமாகி உங்களால் அதை உள்ளே தள்ள முடியாவிட்டால், நீங்கள் உடனடியாக மருத்துவமனையின் அவசர சிகிச்சைப் பிரிவுக்குச் செல்ல வேண்டும். உங்கள் நேர ஒதுக்கீடு விவரங்களை நான் குறித்துக் கொண்டுள்ளேன். உங்கள் ஆலோசனைக் கூட்டத்திற்குப் பிறகு நாங்கள் ஒரு தொடர் அழைப்பை மேற்கொள்வோம். தயவுசெய்து கவனமாக இருங்கள்.
நோயாளி: நன்றி மருத்துவரே. நான் கவனமாக இருப்பேன்.
மருத்துவர் [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: வரவேற்கிறேன் சென்னியப்பா. கவனமாக இருங்கள்.

--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Senniyapp Kumar]] [[AGE|48]] [[GENDER|Male]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-TN-VEL-2024-0815-001]]
Portal [[URL|https://tele.tn.gov.in/visit/APT-TN-VEL-2024-0815-001]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|117.196.20.88]]
Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|353448081234567]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|A4:B5:C6:D7:E8:F9]]
Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9445678901]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|senniyapp.kumar@example.com]]
Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Vellore]]
--- chat ---
Patient: Doctor, I have a problem. For the last two weeks, I have a pain and a small bulge on the right side of my stomach, near my private part. It hurts when I lift heavy things, like the tobacco leaves.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: Hello Senniyapp. I understand your concern. Can you tell me more about the pain? Is it a sharp pain or a dull ache?
Patient: It is a dull ache most of the time, but when I cough or strain, it becomes a sharp pain. I can sometimes push the bulge back in, but it comes out again.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: Thank you for explaining. What you are describing sounds very much like an inguinal hernia. This happens when a part of your intestine pushes through a weak spot in the abdominal muscles.
Patient: Hernia? Is it very serious doctor? Do I need an operation?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: Yes, you will likely need a minor surgery to fix it. The main risk is if the bulge gets stuck and the blood supply is cut off, which is a medical emergency. We need to prevent that from happening.
Patient: I am worried about the cost and taking time off from my work. I am the main earner.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: I understand your worries. The District Hospital Vellore has a general surgery department. They perform many such surgeries, and the cost is minimal for patients like you. You can speak to the hospital's social worker, they will help you with the admission and any financial assistance you may need. For now, you must stop lifting heavy weights and avoid strenuous activity.
Patient: Okay doctor. I will go to the hospital tomorrow. What should I do until then?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: Try to rest as much as possible. If you feel any severe pain, fever, or if the bulge becomes hard and you cannot push it in, you must go to the hospital casualty immediately. I have noted your appointment details. We will have a follow-up call after your consultation. Please take care.
Patient: Thank you, doctor. I will be careful.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: You are welcome, Senniyapp. Take care.

payload.professional_persona: Shabeer Ahmed is a budding horticulture enthusiast who coordinates community clean‑up drives and youth sports events, leveraging their methodical planning and sociable nature to lead small‑scale organic farming projects, while preparing to enroll in a government vocational course to become a rural development assistant.
payload.linguistic_persona: Shabeer Ahmed speaks his native regional language, Urdu, and English fluently in oral form, able to discuss village matters, recite prayers and poetry, and understand basic tech instructions, though they rely on voice‑based apps for reading numbers and signs.
payload.sports_persona: Shabeer Ahmed is an avid footballer leading a weekend village squad and joining informal cricket matches, cheering for the Kolkata Knight Riders in the IPL, and they also mentor younger peers in basic drills.
payload.arts_persona: Shabeer Ahmed enjoys listening to Sufiana Kalam and Qawwali performances, especially those by the legendary Habib Wahab, relishes reciting verses of regional poet Mahjoor, and winds down with Bollywood classics from Lata Mangeshkar and the film *Dilwale Dulhania Le Jaa*.
payload.travel_persona: Shabeer Ahmed has explored nearby Srinagar’s Dal Lake and the bustling markets of Jammu, prefers modest travel with friends by bus, and dreams of trekking the Himalayas of Leh and experiencing Delhi’s historic monuments, balancing adventure with limited funds.
payload.culinary_persona: Shabeer Ahmed enjoys cooking aromatic rogan josh and haakh with plenty of red chilies and fennel, frequently prepares simple yeemuk (a wheat porridge) for breakfast, and delights in tasting jalebi and chaat from roadside stalls, showing a moderate skill level that blends tradition with occasional cravings.
payload.persona: Shabeer Ahmed is a 20‑year‑old village organizer who blends traditional customs with modern tech, juggling football, poetry, horticulture dreams, and a cautious yet curious spirit; they often worry but channel that energy into community projects.
payload.cultural_background: Shabeer Ahmed belongs to a close‑knit Kashmiri Muslim community in the rural hills of Pulwama, Jammu & Kashmir. His family follows Kashmiri customs such as sipping kahwa in winter, attending the weekly jamaat at the local mosque, and celebrating Eid, Navroz and the harvest festival of Baisakhi with traditional music and dances like Rouf. The village culture values mutual help, respect for elders, and a strong oral tradition of storytelling and poetry. While rooted in these age‑old practices, Shabeer is also exposed to satellite TV and smartphones, which fuels his curiosity about new ideas and modern techniques. This blend of heritage and adaptability shapes his everyday outlook.
payload.linguistic_background: Shabeer grew up in a multilingual household where Kashmiri was the language of daily conversation, Urdu was used for religious discourse and formal occasions, and English entered his world through school, television and mobile phones. Although he never learned to read or write in any script, he has strong oral proficiency in all three languages – he can discuss village matters in Kashmiri, recite prayers and poetry in Urdu, and understand basic English instructions, especially those related to technology or government schemes. His functional literacy is limited to recognizing numbers, common symbols on signage, and using voice‑based apps on his mobile phone.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Shabeer’s practical skill set reflects his organized and methodical nature combined with his sociable outlook. He efficiently coordinates small community events such as village clean‑up drives and youth sports matches, ensuring tasks are assigned and deadlines met. He is proficient in vegetable gardening and small‑scale horticulture, applying systematic planting schedules and pest‑control practices learned through observation. In the kitchen, he prepares traditional Kashmiri dishes like rogan josh and haakh, following precise recipes passed down in his family. He possesses basic carpentry skills, able to repair household furniture and build simple wooden tools, and he uses his knack for ‘jugaad’ to devise low‑cost fixes for everyday problems. Additionally, Shabeer is adept at using a mobile phone for voice calls, WhatsApp voice notes, and accessing government scheme information, which complements his strong oral communication abilities.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ['Community coordination', 'Traditional Kashmiri cooking', 'Vegetable gardening', 'Basic carpentry', 'Herbal remedy preparation', 'Mobile phone communication', 'Jugaad problem solving']
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Shabeer enjoys a mix of outdoor and cultural pastimes that match his energetic and sociable nature. He regularly plays football with friends in the village field, and he also participates in informal cricket matches during weekends. He is a keen listener of Sufiana Kalam and often attends local Qawwali sessions at the shrine of Hazrat Sheikh‑ul‑Alam. Reciting Kashmiri poetry, especially the verses of poet Mahjoor, is a favorite pastime that lets him express his emotions. He spends leisure time crafting simple wooden toys for younger children and helping neighbours with garden work. Occasionally, he watches Bollywood movies and regional TV dramas on his mobile, which sparks his curiosity about new ideas. These hobbies keep him physically active and culturally connected.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Playing football', 'Playing cricket', 'Listening to Sufiana Kalam', 'Attending Qawwali gatherings', 'Reciting Kashmiri poetry', 'Crafting wooden toys', 'Watching Bollywood movies on mobile', 'Gardening']
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Shabeer aims to turn his practical abilities into a formal career that benefits his village. In the short term, he plans to enroll in a government‑run vocational course in horticulture or agricultural extension, gaining credentials that will allow him to work as a rural development assistant. He envisions establishing a small organic vegetable farm that can supply local markets and provide a steady income for his family. In the longer term, Shabeer hopes to become a community health or agricultural officer, using his organized approach to introduce sustainable farming practices, improve water management, and coordinate government schemes for youth employment. He also wishes to attend adult literacy classes to acquire basic reading and writing skills, which he sees as essential for advancing his ambitions.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 20‑year‑old male from Pulwama, Jammu & Kashmir, who is never married and has no formal occupation, reports being illiterate with a rural residence. His primary language is Kashmiri, with Urdu used for religious and formal contexts, and he has limited English proficiency. He follows a traditional Kashmiri Muslim lifestyle, consuming kahwa and engaging in community activities, while managing stress through community organizing and horticulture interests.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

Patient presented with a three-week history of productive cough, low-grade fever, and weight loss. Sputum examination revealed acid-fast bacilli, confirming pulmonary tuberculosis. Chest X-ray showed infiltrates in the right upper lobe. The patient was started on a four-drug regimen of Isoniazid, Rifampicin, Pyrazinamide, and Ethambutol as per the Revised National Tuberculosis Control Programme guidelines. Baseline liver function tests and renal function tests were within normal limits. The patient was advised to complete the full six-month course of treatment and to follow up regularly. The patient's mother, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ayesha Bano]], provided consent for the treatment. The patient resides in [[VILLAGE|Chak-e-Hajipora]] and is [[OCCUPATION|Unemployed]].

మోటార్ సైకిల్ ప్రమాదం తర్వాత రోగి ఎడమ ముంజేయికి గాయంతో వచ్చారు. ప్రారంభ పరీక్షలో, బయటి వస్తువుల ప్రమేయం ఉండే అవకాశం ఉన్న లోతైన, ఇన్ఫెక్షన్ సోకిన గాయం కనిపించింది. రోగిని సాధారణ అనస్థీషియా కింద పరిశీలన మరియు శుభ్రపరచడం కోసం ఆపరేషన్ థియేటర్‌కు తీసుకెళ్లారు. ఆపరేషన్ సమయంలో కనుగొన్న విషయాలు 4 సెం.మీ. గాయం, గాజు ముక్కలు మరియు చిన్న రేడియల్ ధమని శాఖ గాయాన్ని ధృవీకరించాయి. గాయం పూర్తిగా కడగబడింది, అన్ని బయటి వస్తువులు తొలగించబడ్డాయి మరియు గాయం పొరల వారీగా కుట్టబడింది. టెటానస్ టాక్సోయిడ్ బూస్టర్ ఇవ్వబడింది. ఆపరేషన్ తర్వాత, రోగికి బ్రాడ్-స్పెక్ట్రమ్ ఇంట్రావీనస్ యాంటీబయాటిక్స్ మరియు నొప్పి నివారణ మందులు ప్రారంభమయ్యాయి. గాయానికి స్టెరైల్ నాన్-అడెరెంట్ డ్రెస్సింగ్ వేయబడింది. రోగి స్థిరంగా ఉన్నారు మరియు పరిశీలనలో ఉన్నారు. గాయం సమీక్ష పెండింగ్‌లో ఉండగా, 48 గంటల్లో డిశ్చార్జ్ ప్లాన్ చేయబడింది.

Patient presented with a traumatic laceration to the left forearm following a motorcycle accident. Initial assessment revealed a deep, contaminated wound with possible foreign body involvement. The patient was taken to the operating theatre for exploration and debridement under general anesthesia. Intra-operative findings confirmed a 4 cm laceration with embedded glass particles and a small radial artery branch contusion. The wound was thoroughly irrigated, all foreign bodies removed, and the laceration repaired in layers. A tetanus toxoid booster was administered. Post-operatively, the patient was started on broad-spectrum intravenous antibiotics and analgesics. The wound was dressed with a sterile non-adherent dressing. The patient is stable and under observation. Discharge planned in 48 hours pending wound review.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER
